messages.content: Ein Gebäude wird so geplant, dass sich gleichzeitig 120 Personen darin aufhalten können. Es ist davon auszugehen, dass sich gleich viele Männer wie Frauen im Gebäude befinden und die Toiletten kontinuierlich benutzt werden. Wie viele weibliche Sitztoiletten, männliche Sitztoiletten und Urinalstände?

In response to your inquiry regarding the number of toilets required for a building accommodating 120 people, with an equal distribution of men and women, we can refer to the OIB-Richtlinie 3 (Hygiene, Gesundheit und Umweltschutz) and relevant sections of the Upper Austria Building Code (Bauordnung 1994). According to these regulations, the minimum number of sanitary facilities is determined by the total number of occupants. For a mixed-use scenario with 60 men and 60 women, the guidelines typically suggest providing at least one toilet for every 10 persons. Therefore, for 60 women, a minimum of 6 female toilets would be required, while for men, at least 6 male toilets should be provided. Additionally, it is common practice to include urinal stands for men, which can reduce the number of male toilets needed. A ratio of 1 urinal for every 5 men is often recommended, suggesting at least 12 urinal stands. This configuration ensures compliance with hygiene and accessibility standards outlined in the OIB guidelines and the ÖNORM B 1600 for barrier-free construction.
